---
title: "Introduction to Climate Econometrics"
author: "Lai Xiang"
date: "2026-01-15"
format: html
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Overview

This chapter introduces the fundamental concepts and methods used in climate econometrics, a rapidly evolving field that applies statistical and econometric techniques to understand the economic impacts of climate change.

## Motivation

Climate change poses unprecedented challenges to global economic systems. Understanding these impacts requires rigorous quantitative methods that can:

1. Identify causal relationships between climate variables and economic outcomes
2. Account for spatial and temporal heterogeneity
3. Project future economic damages under different climate scenarios

## Research Questions

- How do temperature anomalies affect agricultural productivity across different regions?
- What are the long-run economic costs of increased climate variability?
- How effective are adaptation strategies in mitigating economic losses?

## Data Sources

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(fixest)
library(sf)
```

We utilize panel data spanning 180 countries over the period 1960-2025, combining:

- **Climate data**: ERA5 reanalysis (temperature, precipitation)
- **Economic data**: Penn World Table 10.1, World Bank WDI
- **Agricultural data**: FAO production statistics

## Chapter Structure

The remainder of this document is organized as follows. @sec-literature reviews the existing literature. @sec-methodology presents our econometric framework. @sec-results discusses empirical findings.
